wine_bars_late.name: Enoteca Botz
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Natural and low-intervention wines from Italy, Slovenia, France and Spain, with a rotating by-the-glass list strong on macerated whites and chilled sparkling
wine_bars_late.tip: Open to 3am on Friday and Saturday; ask for a chilled macerated white and a plate of seasonal crostini.
wine_bars_late.description: Vanchiglia natural-wine bar and shop pouring small-grower Italian and Central European bottles late into the night, with local seasonal snacks.

wine_bars_late.name: Vincafe
wine_bars_late.address: Via Vittorio Emanuele II 12, 12051 Alba, Italy
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Deep Langhe and Roero list led by Barolo and Barbaresco, with many older vintages by the glass alongside a full kitchen
wine_bars_late.tip: On Alba's central street; perfect for a late Barolo flight after the truffle market closes.
wine_bars_late.description: Rustic-elegant wine bar, cafe and restaurant on Alba's main street, strong on Barolo and Barbaresco by the glass through the evening.

wine_bars_late.name: Ape Wine Bar
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Producer-owned list heavy on local Barbaresco and Barolo, served with tapas-style small plates of Langhe ingredients
wine_bars_late.tip: On Alba's cathedral square; the producer ownership shows in a sharp, value-led by-the-glass list.
wine_bars_late.description: Wine bar on Alba's Piazza Risorgimento owned by local producers, pouring Barbaresco and Barolo until late with small plates of regional fare.

wine_bars_late.name: BER Enoteca
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Small natural and artisanal list with a strong Piedmontese core, by the glass and bottle with a short food menu
wine_bars_late.tip: A neighbourhood natural-wine bar away from the centre; go for the evening service after 6:15pm.
wine_bars_late.description: Cit Turin natural-wine enoteca with a tightly chosen Piedmontese and Italian list poured by the glass into the evening.

wine_bars_late.name: Caffe dell'Orologio
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Natural and traditional wines from Piedmont and beyond, by the glass and bottle, with a small osteria kitchen of seasonal local dishes
wine_bars_late.tip: A neighbourhood San Salvario staple; let the staff steer you through the natural pours alongside the daily kitchen.
wine_bars_late.description: Modern San Salvario wine bar, osteria and shop pouring natural and traditional Piedmontese wines with a seasonal kitchen into the evening.

wine_bars_late.name: Enoteca Mastrovini
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Italian and Piedmontese list by the glass and bottle at the edge of San Salvario by Porta Nuova, open well past midnight on weekends
wine_bars_late.tip: Steps from Porta Nuova station; weekend hours run to 2am, a reliable late stop near the trains.
wine_bars_late.description: San Salvario enoteca by Porta Nuova pouring Piedmontese and Italian wines by the glass, open to 2am Thursday to Saturday.

wine_bars_late.name: Banco Vini e Alimenti
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Globe-spanning natural-wine list with a Piedmontese backbone, paired with a small seasonal kitchen of local produce
wine_bars_late.tip: Low-key Quadrilatero spot; let the staff guide you through the by-the-glass natural pours.
wine_bars_late.description: Charming Quadrilatero Romano natural-wine bar and grocer pouring interesting bottles from Piedmont and beyond with seasonal small plates.

listening_bars.name: Enoteca Vinile
listening_bars.wine_program: Natural and traditional wines by the glass chosen by a music-loving owner, paired with crostini and old vinyl on the turntable
listening_bars.tip: Tell the owner what you are listening to; the wine recommendation usually follows the record.
listening_bars.description: Cosy central-Turin enoteca where natural wines are poured to a soundtrack of the owner's vinyl collection, with crostini to match.

listening_bars.name: Vinile Live Restaurant
listening_bars.address: Strada al Traforo di Pino 106, 10132 Torino, Italy
listening_bars.wine_program: Wine and cocktail list built around live music and DJ nights on the Turin hillside, with a full evening kitchen
listening_bars.tip: Up on the Turin hill; check the gig calendar and book if there is a live set that night.
listening_bars.description: Hillside Turin venue pairing wine and cocktails with live music and DJ sets, a roomier listening-bar option above the city.

listening_bars.name: Smile Tree
listening_bars.wine_program: Research-driven cocktails and a short wine and vermouth list in a tiny design-led room with curated music
listening_bars.tip: Seats are scarce in this micro-bar; arrive early and let the bartender steer between cocktails and vermouth pours.
listening_bars.description: Tiny, design-conscious Quadrilatero bar known for experimental cocktails, vermouth and a curated soundtrack on Piazza della Consolata.

candle_lit.name: Caffe Al Bicerin
candle_lit.wine_program: Historic 1763 cafe pouring vermouth, rosolio and Piedmontese wines beneath mirrored wood panelling and lamplight
candle_lit.tip: The bicerin (coffee, chocolate, cream) is the house ritual; pair an early-evening vermouth before the cafe closes.
candle_lit.description: Candlelit 1763 cafe facing the Consolata sanctuary, with marble tables, mirrored panelling and a vermouth and wine pour list.

candle_lit.name: Vinoteca Centro Storico
candle_lit.wine_program: Handwritten list of Barolo, Barbaresco, Champagne and Burgundy in a tiny low-lit room beneath the Serralunga castle
candle_lit.tip: Friday nights are busiest; the candlelit cellar room under the castle is the most romantic table in the Barolo zone.
candle_lit.description: Intimate, low-lit wine bar below Serralunga castle run by a husband-and-wife team, with prosciutto, a handwritten menu and deep Barolo.

candle_lit.name: Caffe Mulassano
candle_lit.wine_program: Belle-epoque cafe serving its own house vermouth bianco and rosolio delle Alpi at a marble counter under carved ceilings
candle_lit.tip: Order the house Liquore delle Alpi vermouth from the 1879 recipe; the tiny gilded room is best at dusk.
candle_lit.description: Jewel-box 1907 cafe on Piazza Castello, all carved wood and gilt, serving its own house vermouth bianco at the marble counter.

lounges.name: La Drogheria
lounges.wine_program: Sprawling spirits and Turin vermouth selection alongside wines by the glass, a late-night lounge over the great square
lounges.tip: Grab a sofa overlooking Piazza Vittorio Veneto; it runs from early aperitivo straight through to late-night drinks.
lounges.description: Iconic late-night lounge bar on Piazza Vittorio Veneto, mixing Turin vermouth, spirits and wine from aperitivo hour into the small hours.

lounges.name: Affini
lounges.wine_program: One of Turin's widest vermouth and amaro selections alongside a curated wine list, mixed into cocktails or poured neat
lounges.tip: The vermouth wall is the draw; ask the bartender for a Vermouth di Torino flight before dinner tapas.
lounges.description: San Salvario mixology lounge famous for an enormous vermouth and amaro range plus a sharp wine list and creative tapas.

lounges.name: Baratti & Milano
lounges.wine_program: Historic 1875 confectioner and cafe whose founders traded vermouth, syrups and liqueurs, still serving an aperitivo under chandeliers
lounges.tip: Take an early-evening vermouth in the Galleria Subalpina's gilded salon, a Savoy-era lounge in all but name.
lounges.description: Opulent 1875 cafe in the Galleria Subalpina, founded by vermouth and liqueur merchants, serving an aperitivo amid mirrors and chandeliers.

fortified_specialists.name: Casa Martini
fortified_specialists.address: Piazza Luigi Rossi 2, frazione Pessione, 10023 Chieri, Italy
fortified_specialists.wine_program: Home of Martini & Rossi vermouth with guided tastings of the full Vermouth di Torino range and a museum of the aperitivo
fortified_specialists.tip: Book the vermouth class to blend your own bottle; reachable in 20 minutes by train from Turin Porta Susa.
fortified_specialists.description: The Martini & Rossi headquarters near Turin, with a vermouth museum and guided tastings of the full Vermouth di Torino range.

fortified_specialists.name: Caffe Mulassano vermouth counter
fortified_specialists.wine_program: House Vermouth di Torino bianco and Rosolio delle Alpi made to an 1879 recipe, poured at the historic marble bar
fortified_specialists.tip: This is where Italian aperitivo culture took shape; drink the house vermouth bianco standing at the counter.
fortified_specialists.description: The historic Piazza Castello cafe whose 1879 house vermouth bianco and rosolio make it a living shrine to Turin's fortified-wine roots.

fortified_specialists.name: Affini vermouth wall
fortified_specialists.wine_program: Dedicated wall of Turin and Italian vermouths and amari, served neat, on the rocks or built into bespoke cocktails
fortified_specialists.tip: Order a flight straight from the vermouth wall to taste several Vermouth di Torino styles side by side.
fortified_specialists.description: San Salvario bar whose vermouth-and-amaro wall is the best place in Turin to taste a flight of Vermouth di Torino styles late.

late_tastings.name: All'Enoteca
late_tastings.wine_program: Michelin-level Roero and Langhe wine programme tied to a tasting-menu kitchen, with evening flights of Roero Arneis and Nebbiolo
late_tastings.tip: Book the evening service in Canale and let the sommelier pair Roero Arneis and Nebbiolo across the tasting menu.
late_tastings.description: Canale fine-dining address with a serious Roero and Langhe cellar, offering evening wine flights alongside its tasting menu.

late_tastings.name: Rossorubino
late_tastings.wine_program: Large enoteca near Porta Nuova with by-the-glass tastings spanning Piedmont and Italy, plus regular evening tasting flights
late_tastings.tip: A few steps from Porta Nuova; ask about the evening tasting flights to compare Langhe and Monferrato wines.
late_tastings.description: Spacious San Salvario wine shop and enoteca near Porta Nuova, with by-the-glass tastings and evening flights across Piedmont and Italy.

late_tastings.name: Enoteca Fracchia & Berchialla
late_tastings.address: Via Vittorio Emanuele II 17, 12051 Alba, Italy
late_tastings.wine_program: Historic central-Alba wine shop and bar with a vast Langhe range, offering by-the-glass tastings of Barolo and Barbaresco crus
late_tastings.tip: Ask to taste two Barolo crus side by side; the shop's range spans most of the Barolo MGA map.
late_tastings.description: Long-established Alba wine shop and tasting bar in the historic centre, pouring cru Barolo and Barbaresco by the glass into the evening.

late_tastings.name: Vincafe evening Barolo flight
late_tastings.address: Via Vittorio Emanuele II 12, 12051 Alba, Italy
late_tastings.wine_program: Curated evening flights of Barolo and Barbaresco by the glass, including mature vintages, on Alba's central street
late_tastings.tip: Build a vertical of one Barolo cru across vintages; the by-the-glass program runs late into the evening.
late_tastings.description: Vincafe's evening Barolo and Barbaresco by-the-glass flights make it Alba's easiest late-night Nebbiolo tasting on the main street.

sparkling_rooms.name: Enrico Serafino underground cellar
sparkling_rooms.wine_program: Alta Langa traditional-method sparkling refined in 19th-century underground cellars, with guided Metodo Classico tastings
sparkling_rooms.tip: Book the Underground experience to taste Alta Langa where it ages in the 1878 cellars; evenings by reservation.
sparkling_rooms.description: Canale house making since 1878 in its 19th-century underground cellars, with guided tastings of Alta Langa traditional-method sparkling.

sparkling_rooms.name: Coppo Underground Cathedrals
sparkling_rooms.wine_program: UNESCO-listed underground cellars where Coppo ages Metodo Classico sparkling, with guided tastings of its traditional-method wines
sparkling_rooms.tip: Book the Underground Cathedrals tour; the UNESCO cellars run 40m deep and finish on a Coppo Metodo Classico.
sparkling_rooms.description: Coppo's UNESCO-listed Canelli cellars, the underground cathedrals, where the house ages its Metodo Classico sparkling for guided tastings.

sparkling_rooms.name: Casa Martini sparkling and vermouth tasting
sparkling_rooms.address: Piazza Luigi Rossi 2, frazione Pessione, 10023 Chieri, Italy
sparkling_rooms.wine_program: Guided tastings spanning Martini sparkling Asti and Prosecco alongside the Vermouth di Torino range in the historic Pessione cellars
sparkling_rooms.tip: The Discovery Tour pairs Martini's sparkling and vermouth lines; weekends without reservation, weekdays by appointment.
sparkling_rooms.description: Martini & Rossi's Pessione cellars offer guided tastings of its sparkling Asti and Prosecco beside the Vermouth di Torino range.
